content: EURJPY: STOP orders consistently outperform LIMIT across all TP levels. Best: STOP+tp=1.5 (PF=1.22, 106T, 57% WR, DD=3.19%). LIMIT baseline: PF=1.04. Rule: Use STOP for JPY momentum pairs. Tested 2022-2026, 40-test comprehensive sweep.

EURJPY: FVG filter is TOXIC — confirmed 3x (PF 0.71-0.76). Never enable FVG for EURJPY. Tokyo KZ also toxic (PF=0.71). H4Trend=true hurts (PF=0.86). D1=false hurts (0.93). All profiles worse than Custom (Profile=5).

EURJPY: tolerance=80 improves results — PF 1.04→1.16, +8 trades, WR 57→58%, same DD. Wider sweep tolerance catches more valid liquidity sweeps. Default 50 too tight. Not yet combined with STOP+tp=1.5 winner.

USDX (Dollar Index): MacroTrend=false is mandatory. W1 trend classification unreliable for basket instruments. Without: PF=0.00 (0%WR). With: PF=1.41 (15T, 40%WR). Single most important USDX setting. Applies to any basket/index instrument.

USDX: tolerance=100 + MacroOff = best config (PF=1.79, 19T, 42%WR, DD=1.97%). Root cause: USDX price action slow, hasOppositeOB() cancels pending LIMIT before price reaches OB zone. Wider tolerance fixes fill rate. Progression: tol50=1.41, tol80=1.70, tol100=1.79.

USDX: STOP orders do NOT work — PF 0.00-0.74, DD 5-8%. USDX needs retracement LIMIT entries. STOP fills on breakout but USDX breakouts don't follow through. Opposite of EURJPY/GBPJPY where STOP is superior.

Order type selection rule: STOP orders for momentum/JPY pairs (EURJPY, GBPJPY, GBPUSD) — they catch breakout momentum. LIMIT orders for deep-retracement instruments (XAUUSD, USDX) — they need price to retrace into OB zone. XAUUSD: LIMIT mandatory (STOP=2 never triggers). USDX: LIMIT mandatory (STOP PF=0.00-0.74).

Two order cancellation mechanisms in EA: 1) MT5 expiry timer: outdatedOB × PeriodSeconds(CTOB) — e.g. 80×15min=20h (OrderProcess.mqh:103). 2) hasOppositeOB() in cOrderBlock.mqh:107 — cancels pending LIMIT when opposite-direction OB with stars>=3 forms via CancelPendingIfExists() in helpers.mqh:2795. For slow instruments (USDX), mechanism #2 is the dominant cancellation cause. Fix: increase tolerance (not outdatedOB).

GitHub #64: ATR_max/ATR_min use raw iATR() price units. Default 8.2 calibrated only for XAUUSD. For EURUSD/USDJPY/USDX, ATR never reaches 8.2 so filter is 100% inert. Fix needed: normalize to pips via atr[0]/(_Point*10.0). Affects all non-XAUUSD symbols. Forensics ATR_min findings for non-XAUUSD are invalid until fixed.

Pattern for slow/low-volatility instruments (USDX, potentially NAS100): 1) Disable MacroTrend (W1 trend unreliable for baskets/indices). 2) Increase tolerance to 80-100 (price doesn't reach tight OB zones before opposite OB forms). 3) Keep LIMIT orders (breakouts don't follow through). 4) outdatedOB increase has minimal effect — the bottleneck is opposite-OB cancellation, not expiry time.
